title: Holder management — formalize hold-to-SL/TSL/EOD
acceptance_criteria: Create src/trading/management/ directory with HolderManager class
HolderManager.openPosition(tradeIntent, executor, clock) opens a paper trade via PaperTradeExecutor with personality_id set to tradeIntent.personalityId
HolderManager.evaluatePosition(position, currentStraddleValue, clock, triggerConfig) uses the existing evaluateTriggers() from trigger-engine.ts — no duplicate logic
HolderManager.closePosition(position, currentStraddleValue, exitReason, executor) closes via PaperTradeExecutor
ManagementHandler interface exported: { openPosition, evaluatePosition, closePosition } — Adjuster (T-29) and Reducer (T-30) implement this same interface

PositionMonitor refactored: becomes personality-aware. On each straddle.values snapshot, for each open position: (a) loads the position's personality config (cached from startup), (b) dispatches to the correct ManagementHandler based on personality.management_style, (c) calls evaluatePosition — if shouldExit, calls closePosition
PositionMonitor no longer implicitly assumes a single Clockwork personality — it handles all open positions regardless of personality

Startup re-adoption covered: PersonalityRouter.start() (T-27) passes open positions to management handlers; PositionMonitor's existing position loading still works because it queries all open paper_trades
Unit tests: HolderManager.evaluatePosition correctly wraps evaluateTriggers; SL/TSL/TARGET/EOD/DAILY_LOSS all close via closePosition; openPosition writes paper_trades row with correct personality_id
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
